pages.title: Undergraduate Programs | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Business delivers a comprehensive & professional business education, offering a Bachelor of Science degree with 10 concentrations to suit students’ career goals.
contentBlocks: The College of Business (COB) Office of Student Engagement is here to support business students along the entire process of the business student experience at Sac State; from the time you are thinking about applying to the major all the way to when you are graduating — and all the stops along the way.
The College of Business offers a broad professional education in Business. The Bachelor of Science program offers students a choice of 10 concentrations within the business administration major.
Equal Opportunity and Excellence in Education and Employment

pages.title: About the Undergraduate Business program | Sacramento State
pages.description: Academic program information for business majors.
headings: Sequencing of Upper Division Business Major courses matter!
contentBlocks: The College of Business has approximately 4,000 undergraduate business students whom we advise from impacted admissions process through graduation.
Its COB Office of Student Engagement provides accurate, thorough, and concise information with efficiency. Ensuring the timely graduation of our students is a great priority.

All business students must take the major core classes as part of their Business Administration degree. In order to be eligible to enroll in the majority of the major core classes a student must be admitted into the upper division major.

Major core classes should be taken concurrently with concentration courses. Many concentrations have prerequisites from the Major Core. All courses in the lower division/computer literacy requirement, major core and concentrations need to be completed with a minimum C- grade while maintaining a 2.0 GPA in each area.

The College of Business has policies and procedures that are unique to our college. It is the responsibility of the student to be aware of the policies. Please visit the following link for the 3 most common policies. CBA Policies and Procedures.
24 units of coursework in the major core apply to all concentrations.

Students in all concentrations should be aware of and try to take the "gateway" courses in their area as soon as possible, as they impede your ability to progress through the program in a timely manner. Prerequisite requirements for required major core and concentration classes are typically noted in parenthesis in the University Catalog.
The College of Business (COB) offers a broad, professional education in business. Students working toward the BS degree must select at least one of the 10 concentrations that are offered.

In addition to concentration courses, Business Administration students must also complete the Major Core requirements . Concentration courses should be taken concurrently with the Major Core after you enter the major. Many concentrations have prerequisites from the Major Core. In order to advance into the upper division major/concentration, students are required to complete the computer literacy and lower division business requirements. You must submit an application to the upper division major through the Impaction process to move into the upper division major/concentration.
Concentration courses must be completed with a minimum C- grade, while maintaining a 2.0 average in the concentration.
ACCOUNTANCY CONCENTRATION (through Spring 2025)
ACCOUNTANCY CONCENTRATION (effective Fall 2025)
AI MANAGEMENT CONCENTRATION (effective Fall 2026)
FINANCE CONCENTRATION: GENERAL TRACK FINANCIAL PLANNING TRACK REAL ESTATE TRACK RISK MANAGEMENT TRACK
MGMT OF HUMAN RESOURCES & ORGANIZATIONAL BEHAVIOR CONCENTRATION
MANAGEMENT INFORMATION SYSTEMS CONCENTRATION

The College of Business offers various options for student wishing to pursue a minor in Business Administration. The first of the minors offered is a 24 unit minor in Business Administration for a student not majoring in Business Administration. The remaining minors are specialized business minors that are open to business majors and majors outside of Business Administration. A business student may complete a specialized minor as long as it is not the same as their concentration.
AI MANAGEMENT MINOR (effective Fall 2026)
ENTREPRENEURSHIP MINOR (effective Fall 2026)
MGMT OF HUMAN RESOURCES & ORGANIZATIONAL BEHAVIOR MINOR
A student wishing to minor in the College of Business must file a minor request petition.
Whether you began your college career at Sacramento State or transferred from another college (or colleges), all students are required to satisfy:
University General Education & Graduation requirements

Students should familiarize themselves with these requirements as soon as possible in order to plan their college careers. Current General Education and Graduation requirements can be found in the University Catalog .

One way to check on your General Education progress is to review your Academic Requirements in your Student Center . This report attempts to assess your progress in both General Education and in your Major. Please keep in mind that the report may not always accurately read coursework for the Business major if the courses were taken at another institution (or if the transcripts have not been received or were recently received).

If you have questions about General Education Academic Requirements, please see Academic Advising in Lassen Hall. Questions related to the Business major should be directed to the Undergraduate Advising Center in Tahoe Hall 1030, (916) 278-2499.

Please keep in mind that at least 9 units of General Education (3 courses) must be completed at the Upper Division level (approved junior/senior level classes at Sacramento State numbered "100-199"). The list of approved courses can be found in the University Catalog.

This means that even if you completed all of your General Education "Area" requirements (A,B,C,D,E) at another college, at least 3 more courses must be completed at Sacramento State (courses must be approved General Education courses numbered 100-199). At least one of the 3 courses must be an approved "Writing Intensive" course.
All lower division and upper division business courses must be passed with a C- or better with the exception of MATH 24 which requires a minimum acceptable grade of “C”.

Students whose Sac State or overall GPA falls below 2.0 may be disqualified from the business major. As a result, they will not be able to enroll in any business courses until they have been readmitted.

The Academic Standards Policy of the College of Business requires all disqualified students to reapply (i.e., submit an application) to the business major once they are back in good standing with the university. Students seeking readmission will be ranked by their Sacramento State and lower division business GPAs among all other applicants through the impaction process.

It is rare for a business major to be short of the 120 unit requirement for graduation with a bachelor’s degree. It may happen in rare cases when a student has been very efficient choosing general education courses along with their major courses. In order to be sure you will reach 120 units, all you need to do it determine the units you have remaining and then add that to the number of units you have completed.

You can see how many units you have completed by reviewing your Unofficial Transcript in your Student Center . Be sure to account for repeats and transfer credit that might not yet have transferred when calculating your units.

College of Business lower division and upper division major requirements may be repeated twice (for a total of three [3] attempts – 2nd and 3rd attempts will be averaged). If a required course is not completed with a minimum acceptable grade in three attempts, the student may not continue in the business major.
Upper Division courses used to satisfy graduation requirements must have been completed within ten (10) years of the date of graduation.

links.text: MGMT OF HUMAN RESOURCES & ORGANIZATIONAL BEHAVIOR CONCENTRATION

pages.description: Information for business majors regarding academic advising, major planning, status of degree.
headings: OSE Hours & Where to Find Us
Extended Drop-in Advising During Peak Registration
Scheduling an Appointment with an Advisor
How do I file to graduate?
When will I receive my diploma?
contentBlocks: Advising in the COB is a dual activity between the UBAC and the College's faculty.
Questions relating to concentrations and career information should be addressed to a faculty advisor.

The Office of Student Engagement will be holding in person and virtual office hours for students who have any questions pertaining to our services. Drop-in advising is designed t o assist students with quick questions, next semester scheduling, and any additional help that does not require a scheduled appointment.
Wednesday, April 29, 9:00 am - 12:00 p;m

Academic Advising in the Office of Student Engagement is available both in person and virtually. We are also happy to answer any questions for you via email. If you wish to reach out to more than one advisor please include all the advisors in one email rather than sending individual emails. Please see email addresses below:
If your LAST name begins with A - M please email Stephanie Serrato Neumann ( sjs256@csus.edu )
If your LAST name begins with N - Z please email Maria Lindstrom ( mariar@csus.edu )

UBAC counselors meet with students interested in finding out the status of their degree or in planning their curriculum, as well as students on Academic Probation. Only students who are currently enrolled as an upper division Business Major or "Expressed Interest" in Business on My Sac State may schedule an individual appointment with an Undergraduate Business Advising Center Academic Counselor. All other students who are not able to change their status to Expressed Interest may stop by the UBAC during scheduled zoom drop-in advising or ask quick questions during our virtual office hours.

Appointments are currently available virtually and in person. Appointments for business advising occur on a weekly basis. The appointments open on Fridays after 3:00 for the following week. If you have submitted a graduation application for Spring 2026 or Fall 2026 or are planning on submitting a graduation application for Spring 2026 or Fall 2026 you do not need to schedule a long term appointment. Please come to drop-in advising or email your advisor (see above). Please click on the link below to schedule the appointment.

There will be limited appointments April 13 - April 30. Appointments will be available with Peer Advisors only. The COB Advisors will be focusing on drop-in advising during the peak registration time to accommodate as many students as possible.
Please use the info below when scheduling an appointment through the link below:
Service: College of Business and choose Business Advising OR if you are Expressed Interest in Business you can choose EI in Business Advising
Pick a Date: Choose a date and will see available dates for the week
Schedule an appointment with a business advisor
If you have career questions of a specific concentration please visit a faculty advisor.
Transfer PowerPoint (Expressed Interest)
Apply to graduate online through Student Center on My Sac State once 85 units completed
Commencement information can be found at www. csus.edu/commencement
Students who are graduating in the summer may participate in Spring.
Your diploma will be mailed approximately 6 months after graduation. Students who need proof of their degree should order transcripts .

pages.title: Impacted Major Information | Sacramento State
pages.description: Learn about the requirements for applying to the undergraduate program in the College of Business, which is currently in impacted status.
headings: Associate's Degree for Transfer (ADT) in Business Administration
contentBlocks: The Chancellor's Office of the California State University has granted the College of Business impaction status for its undergraduate business major. All students who apply to Sacramento State as business majors are admitted as expressed interest in business students.
Students applying to the business major are required to submit a supplemental business application.
Learn More about University Level Impaction
Overall, students must have 45 units completed with a qualifying overall grade point average (minimum 2.4 GPA ); and be in good standing at last college or university attended.

Please note: merely have a 2.4 GPA does not guarantee admission to upper division business program. Students will be rank-ordered by overall GPA. The GPA cutoff for the past few admission cycles has been around the 2.8 range but does fluctuate.
Students must have completed the following courses with minimum C- grades with the exception of MATH 24 which must be completed with minimum C grade prior to applying:
MATH 24: Modern Business Math Calculus courses ok in lieu of MATH 24. Pre-calculus and Finite Math do not meet the MATH 24 requirement.
Pre-calculus and Finite Math do not meet the MATH 24 requirement.
MIS 10: Business Applications (Spreadsheets)
New applicants, including transfer students, must have been admitted to the university prior to applying to the upper division major.

The Supplemental Business Application can be found on the online Student Center on My Sac State. When you first log into your Student Center you will want to click on "Switch to Full Site" and once at the Full Site you will go to the bottom right corner under "Forms" and click on Supplemental Application. (see below) You can also click here to watch a video with directions on accessing and completing the supplemental application.
Unofficial transcripts of all course work outside of Sacramento State must be submitted to the Undergraduate Business Advising Center in the Office of Student Engagement.
A supplemental application must still be submitted in order to be considered for the major.
Local area applicants are not guaranteed admission to the major.
Students identified as a local area applicant by the university will be awarded a 0.1 GPA boost to their cumulative GPA.
The GPA boost will be in addition to and/or separate from the GPA boost that students completing an ADT will receive.
ADT benefits only available for student's applying to Sacramento State in first semester.
Students earning the ADT must still submit a supplemental application to the major.
Students who are earning an ADT in Business Administration may apply to the upper division business major with work in progress.
Students earning the ADT will earn a 0.1 GPA boost for admission purposes.
The ADT curriculum is used in lieu of the Business major specific courses for admission purposes. Missing courses must still be completed for graduation purposes.
The ADT in Business Administration does not guarantee admission to the major.

If you are pursuing a ADT in Business Administration, a verification from your community college that the ADT is in progress must be received by the Sacramento State admissions office by their posted deadline.
Associate's Degree for Transfer (ADT) in Business Administration

pages.description: Commonly sought for resources for undergraduate students in the College of Business.

pages.title: Office of Student Engagement | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Business Office of Student Engagement offers advising support to future and current business major students.
headings: OSE Staff Share Student Success Tips
contentBlocks: The Office of Student Engagement exists to make your time at Sac State a productive one by providing services essential to the success of our College of Business students (or soon to be students)!
It is our goal to make all business students feel welcomed and supported outside of the classroom where learning continues to take place!

We provide special events for business students throughout the academic year and specifically during key times in the semester. Our events are designed to connect students within the College of Business Administration as well as with the local business community to refine skills for future career success!
Join us for free coffee and donuts at our kick-off event Crash the OSE during the first week each semester and check out the OSE Schedule of Events to see everything we have in store for you!

Please view our New Student Guidebook to gain a comprehensive overview of the Business Administration major, minor, and concentrations. This guidebook will assist you in planning out your time with the College of Business and put you on a path to success.

Please view our OSE Programs Guidebook to learn about the numerous programs and services we provide to all business students. We provide a broad range of programs to support our students, ranging from academic success and support, professional development and networking and engaging ways to connect with peers, employers and the College of Business

links.text: OSE Staff Share Student Success Tips

pages.title: Office of Student Engagement | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Business Administration Office of Student Engagement offers advising support to future and current business major students.
contentBlocks: The BizList is our listserv email communication to keep business students informed and up to date. Business students are encouraged to subscribe to our BizList to get updates and important announcements.

The CBA Office of Student Engagement sends out email notifications on a regular basis, regarding internships, advising, important dates for applications, job & internship announcements for both on and off campus opportunities, scholarships, etc.
Subscribe below and make sure to select the BUSADM-L list.
Find out what's happening in the OSE and use our new online registration system (VisitDays) to sign up for the events you want to attend!

VisitDays requires only one sign-up - after that, just click the "register" button on the event of your choosing, and you're in! You will then receive a unique QR code for you to use to check-in at our events. All OSE events will be listed on VisitDays and updated periodically, so check back often!

pages.title: BizGen1 Program | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Business Office of Student Engagement offers a BizGen1 Program to support our First Generation Business Students.
contentBlocks: BizGen1 is a peer mentorship program specifically designed to support first-generation business students within Sac State's College of Business. Coordinated by the COB Office of Student Engagement, the program's goal is to help first-gen biz students build community, access key resources, and thrive throughout their academic journeys.

If you're a first-generation business student, welcome to the BizGen1 community - your journey starts here. Whether you have questions, need support, or want to meet other first-generation business students, BizGen1 is here for you! Stop by our office (TAH 1030), attend a BizGen1 event, or schedule a meeting with one of your peer mentors—we’re excited to support your journey in the College of Business, and we can’t wait to see what you achieve!
A BizGen1 student is either “Expressed Interest” or currently declared within the business major, and meets one of the following first-generation student qualifying criteria:
Neither natural nor adoptive parent(s) has earned a bachelor’s degree
You lived with and received support from only one parent before age 18, and that parent did not earn a bachelor’s degree
Before age 18, you did not regularly live with nor receive support from either natural or adoptive parent(s)
BizGen1 Peer Mentors support your success by offering:
Updates on events and opportunities for first-gen business students
One-on-one mentoring (in person or via Zoom, by appointment)
Warm referrals to campus departments, with follow-up support
Aaralyn Si Brief Intro (Aaralyn) Email Aaralyn
Ron Baptiste Brief Intro (Ron) Email Ron
BizGen1 events are tailored especially for first-generation business students. They offer opportunities to:
Build connections with faculty, staff, and peers
Explore student clubs and engagement opportunities
Learn about academic support and career resources
Strengthen your sense of belonging in the College of Business
BizGen1 events are shared via email, our BG1 Canvas course and Instagram, @cob_bizgen1
If you qualify as a BizGen1 student, but have not received our emails, nor been invited to join the BG1 Canvas course, please contact one of your BizGen1 Peer Mentors.

The BizGen1 GPS ( G uiding your P ath to S uccess) Program is designed to expand your network, connect you with student success resources, and help you become more engaged with both the College of Business and Sac State as a whole.

Housed within its own unique Canvas course, the program spans nearly the full length of the spring semester, giving students the freedom and flexibility to complete tasks when it is most convenient for their schedules. Additionally, three levels of completion allow students to adjust their goals as needed, and all those who participate are invited to an end of year celebration.
For more information or to join the program, please contact one of your BizGen1 Peer Mentors.
📍 COB Office of Student Engagement, Tahoe Hall, Room 1030

pages.title: Business-Affiliated Clubs & Organizations (Biz Clubs) | Sacramento State
pages.description: Information regarding student organizations affiliated with the Business Administration major.
contentBlocks: The College of Business connects with several business-affiliated student clubs (we call them Biz Clubs! ) that provide social and professional networking, leadership opportunities, career-enhancing events, meetings, and fun activities. Biz Clubs offer something for everyone, so get involved today!

We hear time and time again, from both recruiters and students, that it is the leadership developed with Biz Clubs that sets individuals apart when entering the workforce. Within the first couple weeks of every semester, we host an event for students to explore and connect with our business clubs: Biz Fest in the fall and Biz Fling in the spring. Check out our Events for Business Students page for more information about these and all OSE events.

pages.title: Internships & Job Board | Sacramento State
pages.description: Information for business majors regarding internships, academic internships and resources for identifying opportunities, as well as for employers seeking to hire business students for opportunities in their organizations.
contentBlocks: The information below pertains only to internships where a declared business major earns academic credit for an approved internship experience. below for important dates regarding documentation submission for Fall, Spring and Summer Academic Internships. These dates are non-negotiable as there are internal deadlines set by the University. Summer Academic Internship deadlines are subject to summer term census dates. If a student is interested in receiving academic credit for an internship, we suggest reaching out to us as soon as possible to start the process or with any questions.

Expressed Interest business students are not eligible for an academic internship. At any time, any business student is allowed to, and encouraged, to complete internships without receiving academic credit.

The College of Business does not offer internship placement. Students are responsible for obtaining an internship on their own prior to starting the process outlined below. Internship opportunities can be found in the Internship Sources section of this page.

Note: Academic credit earned for an academic internship in the COB will always be 3.0 units and graded on a Credit/No Credit basis. These credits do not satisfy any major, core or elective credit requirements – they are additional credits. Internship courses can only be taken one time.
The steps below can also be downloaded at the link below.
The student must verify that all requirements have been met and submit initial paperwork for the application to be reviewed.

COB Student Internship Application Faculty Recommendation Form The Faculty Recommendation form must be completed by a business Faculty member (part-time or full-time); however, they do not need to be within the student’s concentration. Resume Offer Letter from the employer Job Description from employer Must be in line with the student's concentration Unofficial CSUS Transcript

Faculty Recommendation Form The Faculty Recommendation form must be completed by a business Faculty member (part-time or full-time); however, they do not need to be within the student’s concentration.
The Faculty Recommendation form must be completed by a business Faculty member (part-time or full-time); however, they do not need to be within the student’s concentration.
Job Description from employer Must be in line with the student's concentration
Must be in line with the student's concentration

Once all initial paperwork is submitted and preapproved, the student will be emailed the Student Learning Agreement. This must be signed by the student's employer and Faculty Sponsor. Once completed, the student will submit the agreement through email to the OSE Administrative Analyst.

Faculty Sponsor (Different than the Faculty recommendation) This will be the faculty member that will act as the student’s “Instructor” throughout the semester, who the student will report to, and who will assign a grade at the end of the semester. The Faculty Sponsor must be a full-time faculty member in the subject the student is requesting credit for. Example: If a student is requesting ACCY 195 enrollment, their sponsor must be a full-time accounting faculty member.
This will be the faculty member that will act as the student’s “Instructor” throughout the semester, who the student will report to, and who will assign a grade at the end of the semester.

The Faculty Sponsor must be a full-time faculty member in the subject the student is requesting credit for. Example: If a student is requesting ACCY 195 enrollment, their sponsor must be a full-time accounting faculty member.
Example: If a student is requesting ACCY 195 enrollment, their sponsor must be a full-time accounting faculty member.
The Faculty Sponsor and the faculty member completing the Faculty Recommendation Form can be the same person only if they are a full-time faculty member in the student’s concentration.

Once the student submits their completed Student Learning Agreement, all documentation will be submitted for signature and final approval by the Director of the OSE and Associate Dean of Academic Programs. If approved, the student will be manually enrolled into the appropriate section by the Enrollment Specialist. Once enrolled, the student will receive an email from the OSE Administrative Analyst with steps to follow throughout the semester.

The Business Job Board allows you to search and view positions by several different search features and it also allows you to set up job alerts so you can stay informed about the types of opportunities you are most interested in seeing.
You can also view employer profiles to learn about them, and follow links to their websites for further information or to research before an interview.
You can also apply to the job/internship directly through the Business Job Board.
Employers post new positions on a regular basis, so be sure to visit the site often to see new opportunities.

The Business Job Board allows you to post, modify, close and/or reopen positions that you would like to announce to currently enrolled business students at Sacramento State. Once you create an employer profile and we activate your account, you will be able to log in and post internship and job opportunities for Sac State Business Students.

Once your position is posted, you will receive an electronic confirmation, and your position will be listed on our website. Students will be able to apply through the job board and you will be able to monitor and track applicants who are in your hiring pipeline.

Visit the Business Job Board and start by creating an employer account and then post as many positions as you have available. You can log back in at any time with the user-name and password you will select.
COB Academic Internship Student Information

pages.title: Scholarships for Business Students | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Business Office of Student Engagement offers advising support to future and current business major students.
contentBlocks: The College of Business offers nearly 50 scholarships totaling over $300,000 in student scholarship awards to our business majors each school year. Business majors are encouraged to apply every year. Our scholarship application is available between October to March each year, with all scholarships awarded at the beginning of the next academic year.
College of Business departmental scholarships are available to business majors in all concentrations and are awarded to Business students each academic year.
Gather your documents (e.g. resume, transcripts, etc.) and items that you may need to reference while completing the online form.
Using the "APPLY NOW" link on the CSUS Scholarship Application page, begin to complete your General Scholarship Application.
Monitor your Scholarship account for any notifications for additional scholarship documents you are required to provide; this includes specialized essays, transcripts, resumes, etc
The COB Scholarships are offered once per year with one application period per year.
COB Scholarships for the 2025-2026 academic year have been awarded.
The application period for COB Scholarships for the 2026 - 2027 academic year will be open October 1, 2025 through March 2, 2026

pages.title: Business Honor Societies | Sacramento State
pages.description: Information regarding honor societies affiliated with the Business Administration major.
contentBlocks: Business students may also be invited to an Honors organization based on their academic performance and other eligibility criteria. The CBA currently recognizes the following Honors organizations:

Founded in 1913, lifetime membership in Beta Gamma Sigma, the International Business Honor Society is the highest recognition a student can receive in a business program accredited by AACSB International. Admitted business majors who are in the top 10% of their class and graduate students in the top 20% of their class whom also meet additional eligibility criteria are invited to become lifetime members of BGS twice annually, before the fall and spring semesters. For full eligibility criteria and more information on the Sac State Chapter view the Beta Gamma Sigma Breakdown .

The Phi Kappa Phi (PKP) All-University Honor Society, founded in 1897 and now located on more than 250 American campuses, is Sacramento State's oldest honor society. Members are selected from all areas of the university on the basis of academic excellence. Membership is by invitation only: students must meet GPA and completed unit requirements in order to be eligible. New members are initiated each Spring.

Golden Key is the world’s largest collegiate honor society, recognizing outstanding academic achievement and connecting high-achieving individuals locally, regionally & globally with lifetime opportunity, reward & success. Membership is by invitation only and applies to the top 15% of college and university sophomores, juniors and seniors, as well as top-performing graduate students in all fields of study, based solely on their academic achievements. The purpose of Golden Key is to recognize academic excellence and improve the community through service events. For more information visit www.goldenkey.org .

pages.title: About the Outstanding Student Awards | Sacramento State
pages.description: The university and the College of Business enjoy recognizing the success of its students through a series of awards programs.
contentBlocks: Each year the COB recognizes and awards Outstanding Student Awards to both undergraduate and graduate students. Eligible students are notified by email from the Office of Student Engagement; this happens in January and this serves as an invitation to apply for these awards.
COB Outstanding Student Awards are open only to those who receive a letter of invitation . All students meeting minimum criteria may apply for Dean’s Awards consideration.
Outstanding MS/Business Analytics Student
Each spring semester, the university hosts an award process for a Dean’s Award from each academic college.
who have a graduation date during that current academic year (Fall, Spring or Summer) and

are invited to apply for this prestigious award. Applicants are judged not only on their excellent academic achievements, but also by their contributions to the campus and community and obstacles faced and overcome on their path to a college degree. One student is selected from all College Dean's Award recipient to receive the campus-wide President’s Medal for that academic year.

pages.title: Google Career Certificates | Sacramento State
pages.description: Information about Google Career Certificates offered through the Sacramento State College of Business
contentBlocks: The College of Business has established a two-year partnership with Coursera to offer six Google Professional Certificates. This gives us the opportunity to provide access to Google Professional certificates to COB Students, Alumni, Staff, Faculty, and Administrators. This includes Faculty using one or more of the certificate programs (including micro-credentials in each certificate program) for a course they teach. The partnership will be in effect from January 2023 - December 2027. To maximize participation, seats will be allocated on a 6-month term for individuals and by semester for course-related engagement. Extensions may be allowed, based on demand and availability

The 8 Professional Certificate Programs offered through this partnership are self-paced. For more complete descriptions, please view each short description below and follow the links for more details, information, career opportunities, typical income ranges, and more.

Google Career Certificates provide people with access to in-demand jobs through rapid reskilling without the need for a college degree or prior experience in the fields of IT Support, IT Automation Data Analytics, Project Management, Digital Marketing & e-Commerce, and User Experience (UX) Design.
These certificates were developed by and are taught by Google employees, and are hands-on, practical, and rigorous. They can be completed in under six months on a part-time basis.

Designed by Google and sponsored by your school, this technology-based program can prepare you for a career in these high-growth fields. Once you complete a Google Career Certificate, you'll be connected to resources to help you jump-start your job search.
Why wait? There are over 1.3 million job openings across the field of technology and the demand for qualified workers is growing.
Sign up to participate in the #GrowWithGoogle program in the College of Business. Spots are offered in 5-6 month increments.
Important Note: Do not try to enroll directly into the course from the links in the information pages below.
To enroll in one or more of the certificate program, at no charge, as part of the partnership with the COB and Google/Coursera:
The College of Business will enroll interested individuals into our program, on a first-come, first-served basis.
Once enrolled in the COB #GrowWithGoogle program, you will have access to all six Google Certificate programs and choose the certificate(s) you want to complete.
Complete this Grow With Google Interest Form to secure your spot today!

Do you like to identify trends? Are you curious about how things work? Prepare for a new career in the high-growth field of data analytics in under six months; no experience is required. You’ll get professional training designed by Google and the opportunity to connect with top employers currently hiring.
Learn more about the Google Data Analytics Certificate
Reminder: Do not attempt to enroll in the course directly once on the information page.

Do you like connecting with people online and building an online presence? Prepare for a new career in the high growth fields of digital marketing and e-commerce in under six months, no experience required. You’ll get professional training designed by Google and gain hands-on experience using popular tools, such as Canva, Constant Contact, Hootsuite, HubSpot, Mailchimp, Shopify, Twitter, Google Ads, and Google Analytics.
Learn more about the Google Marketing & e-Commerce Certificate
Do you like solving problems and helping others?

Prepare for a new career in the high-growth field of IT support in under six months, with no experience required. You’ll get professional training designed by Google, along with the opportunity to connect with top employers that are currently hiring.
Learn more about the Google IT Support & Automation Certificate

Do you like solving problems, staying organized, and working with people? Prepare for a new career in the high-growth field of project management in under six months, no experience is required. You’ll get professional training designed by Google and the opportunity to connect with top employers currently hiring.
Learn more about the Google Project Management Certificate

Do you like creating solutions, understanding people, and organizing? Prepare for a new career in the high-growth field of user experience (UX) design in under six months, with no experience required. You’ll get professional training designed by Google, along with the opportunity to connect with top employers that are currently hiring.
Learn more about the Google UX Design Certificate

Want to use generative AI tools but not sure where to start? Google Prompting Essentials teaches you how to give clear and specific instructions to generative AI - known as prompting. In 5 easy steps, you'll learn how to prompt effectively and unlock more of AI’s benefits.
Learn more about the Google Prompting Essentials Certificate

Googl AI Essentials is a self-paced course designed to help people across roles and industries get essential AI skills to boost their productivity, zero experience required. The course is taught by AI experts at Google who are working to make the technology useful for everyone.
Learn more about the Google AI Essentials Certificate

contentBlocks: Semester Honors are awarded and the notation "Deans Honors List" is posted to the permanent academic record for freshmen earning a 3.0 GPA and other undergraduates earning a 3.25 GPA. To be eligible in either category, students must complete a minimum of 12 letter-graded units with no incomplete grades and no grade lower than a C-.
Graduate students are not eligible for the Dean's Honors List
Each semester, we invite students who earn a spot on the Dean's Honor List to stop by and take a photo with the Dean of the College of Business.
Each semester, the College of Business publishes our Dean's Honor List. To view a current or past semester, please click below.

pages.title: Professional Pathways: Student Conversations with Business Leaders | Sacramento State
pages.description: Virtual networking and mentoring between business leaders and business students.
headings: Professional Pathways: Student Conversations with Business Leaders
contentBlocks: We want to connect you with business leaders to help you learn more about how to navigate your next professional steps, expand your network and benefit from professional mentoring during this time and where available, make employment connections.

During the Fall semesters, we will invite you to engage with Business leaders in to network and via phone, email, Zoom or in-person. Students enrolled into the program will be matched with at minimum one Business professional for one 30-minute conversation; you and the business professional can continue beyond that if further connection is mutually desired.
Our next Professional Pathways will be offered in the Fall 2025 semester.

Your responses will be compiled and utilized to determine best matches between you and Business Leaders based off both the student and professional registration forms. A combination of factors will be taken into account, most specifically shared topics of interest identified, industry/concentration areas and hiring interests.

Note: Professionals you are matched with may be in another field or industry than you are pursuing. The ability to make direct matches will be based off both student and employer registrations which we cannot predict in advance. We encourage everyone to have an open mind, as we know from experience, these professionals will still have great wisdom to share and be able to add value to your personal journey!
The COB Office of Student Engagement will form and communicate the paring(s) between Business Leaders and Business students.

Initial introductions are made by email from the OSE to both the student and the Business Professional, to share contact info and preferred mode(s) of communication (phone, email, Zoom or in person) as identified by the registration information provided. You will receive a separate pairing email for each professional you are matched with if you are assigned more than one.
The student should initiate contact with their professional paring(s) within 5 business days of pairing emails being disseminated.
Students should reference the contact method preferred as provided in the paring email(s).
Students and the business professional then should determine a day/time and method to connect.
Conversations should be completed by the program end date.
Note: It is at the discretion of the student and professional if they would like to continue further with any additional conversations outside of the one allotted for this program.
Students should complete the Professional Pathways post-event survey after they have completed their conversation(s). This will be emailed out at a later date.
Registration for the Fall 2025 Professional Pathways will open in September 2025.
Are you an employer, industry professional or Alumni who would like to get involved? Please use the link below to register.

links.text: Professional Pathways: Student Conversations with Business Leaders

pages.title: Questions for the Council | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Business Advisory Council members' responses to student-submitted questions.
headings: What is the best thing a student can do to set themselves apart as a highly desired future employee?
Robert Rivinius, Executive Director, Family Business Association of California
Virginia Varela, President and CEO, Golden Pacific Bank
What traits or skill do you hope students have after graduation?
Gail Jones, Senior Business Advisor to Chief Human Resources Office, VSP Global
Michael Fabbrini, Pacific Region Director of Finance for UNFI
What skills should we focus on as students as we approach our virtual work?
Will there be more remote options for employment moving forward as a result of COVID-19?
Jill Brown, President, Principal Funds Distributor
Bill O'Keefe, Executive Vice President, InterWest Insurance Services, LLC
How do you stay motivated when working from home?
Whenever things go back to normal, do you expect employees that can work from home to continue working from home or would you welcome them back to the office?
Chelsea Minor, Corporate Director for Consumer & Public Affairs for Raley’s
Debra Sleigh, Executive, Adventist Health IT, Cerner Corporation
First Time Working in a Remote Setting
What is the best advice you can give to someone who is entering the workforce for the first time while working from home?
JoDee Harrison, Senior Vice President, Wells Fargo
contentBlocks: This is an ongoing forum for the COB Advisory Council members to respond to student submitted questions and provide valuable advice, mentoring and guidance to business students for continued professional development.

Set yourself apart from the herd by having a very good resume and trying to contact people. Network as much as you can. Sending out 1,000 resumes to job listings will probably result in 0 interviews or jobs, especially with no work experience. Work harder at it than the next guy.

It would be great if a student could get some real live work experience in the field of their choice. Many students have scholastic achievements and a set of low skill jobs but trying something that requires flexibility and decision making and project skills will be of great use. Also, be sure that you have the basics down such as preliminary math and accounting skills, economic understanding, etc., and stay up on current events. You would be surprised how impressive you will sound if you can weave examples into your discussion of global and national current events that impact our market.
Regardless of the industry, candidates who have and sustain the following traits/skills rise to the top (in no particular order):
Excellent verbal and written communication
Analytical, Critical thinking and Problem resolution

Communication skills are so important, both written and verbal. Being able to professionally and concisely communicate is at the top of my list. There’s a quote… “I’m sorry I wrote you a long letter, I didn't have time to write you a short one” and it’s so true. With executives receiving hundreds of emails a day, spending time reading an email that is paragraphs long is a pet peeve, and in addition, every email should be drafted as if it were to be read by the CEO. Emails are forwarded to groups to provide updates to teams.

I look for people that are very hard working and are willing to volunteer for new assignments or working groups. People that volunteer tend to learn about their new organization faster than other new hires and tend to get promoted more quickly.
New employees who are willing to learn and ask questions. If you don’t know about something, ask! Don’t be shy.
Integrity and honesty. Always be honest and forthright, even its uncomfortable.
Communication skills are the most important in the virtual world. It is important to represent yourself well in both verbal and written communication.
Take time to think about the question before you answer it.
When writing, always review your work to ensure you answered the question and that spelling, and grammar are correct before hitting the send button.
Remember to write to your audience, and be as detailed as need be if the audience is not part of your group.

To be successful when working virtually, I would recommend focusing on communication and project management skills. Communication is essential in order to make sure that the work you are doing is on track and that you will not let your coworkers or teammates down.

I would recommend taking the time to make sure you understand the deadlines and deliverables and understand how your portion will fit into the larger project or what the client/customer’s expectations are. Too many times, I have had an item develop into a crisis that could have been avoided by asking more questions up front to make sure that everyone was on the same page.

Your manager or supervisor may or may not be a good communicator — take the initiative to ask questions so that you facilitate the communication. Also checking in on a regular basis and providing a progress update will help to keep others informed and also if you are going down the wrong path, will allow others to get you back on track, saving time and energy for the entire team.

Project management skills are essential — in a typical office environment, you might have more visible deadlines or be able to observe where the team is at on a large project (but) when working remotely, it is harder to ascertain project status. I have found that if you can spend some time up front mentally creating the steps in the project and thinking about each one in detail, it is easier to set a timeline and a deadline for each step and keep everyone motivated to get the project to completion. Too often, we jump right into the project in order to start it, rather than taking a pause to think about the steps and mentally go through them to determine what is needed at each step and then line up the resources so they are available when needed. In today’s fast paced world, I think it is easy to “move” rather than “think/plan” and it ends up taking more time later on than a small upfront investment.

Yes, I believe we will have more remote working/hiring opportunities. I have hired three people since COVID started — all in different locations. I feel COVID has expanded the diversity of talent that I am able to interview for opportunities.

Depending on the industry, I expect there will be more options available for remote employment. Given Covid-related work restrictions, we have been forced to acquire more robust work from home tools. Consequently, at InterWest we are allowing more employees to work from home either on a full or part time basis. As another example, my daughter and her husband are both employed in digital marketing positions and both work from home for employers who are nominally headquartered in S.F. and N.Y. with no plans to ever require working from an office location.
Maintain a focus on meeting deadlines, driving results and producing quality work. Have the mindset that my colleagues count on me and the work I produce; therefore, I do not want to let them down.

I am a big user of lists and I try to start each day with a plan of what I would like to accomplish. Not only do I get the satisfaction of completing the items on the list, but it helps keep me focused on what I set out to get done that day and prevents interruptions from eating into my plan. Sometimes the interruptions are important and I need to adjust and move a few items to another day, but I am aware of what I am deferring and can make a conscious choice in the moment. I also think taking some breaks in the day to get outside, even if only to get a coffee or check the mailbox helps to refresh one mentally.

There is nothing that can replace in-person interactions. Many companies have built their culture on “collaboration” and will depend on in-person interactions to get the most out of their employees. With that said, companies will be more creative and flexible in their requirements for office work.
There will be a hybrid model as we make the transition back to work. In this scenario, not every person will be in the office every day, but rather select days for meetings and workload.
There will be more flexibility for work/life balance and more opportunities for an employee to work from home for family or other life issues.
There may be some smaller companies who do not come back to the office and require remote work full-time.
As a student, you should think about the environment that you prefer and ask these questions of your future employer.

Before COVID changed our working model, many believed that staff were more productive with physical oversight. Since last March, we have seen associates move immediately into a work from home model and not only succeed in keeping productivity levels satisfactory, but exceeded expectations. We could not have done this without the quality of today's virtual tools and expect the future to become a hybrid model. There are still times when planning, team formation/orientation, performance reviews and celebrations are better done in person. So eventually, I predict employers will shrink their expensive real estate footprints and go hybrid (working from home with hotel cubicles for those who must come together).

For those entering the work force while working from home, the best advice I can provide is make sure you are reaching out to others for support. Not being in the office can deeply affect collaboration and the learning curve for new employees. Work with your leader to establish a peer coach that can be a go to person. Make sure to calendar weekly/monthly 1-on-1s to ensure there is a forum to monitor progress and have questions answered. Learning and development in the job should be driven by the individual and supported by a leader or manager. Take control of your success!

links.text: First Time Working in a Remote Setting

pages.title: Resources for Virtual Networking, Employment and Professional Development | Sacramento State
pages.description: Resource page for information, tips, opportunities for Business Administration students trying to navigate the virtual workplace environment.
headings: Resources for Virtual Networking, Employment and Professional Development
Top 15 Websites to Find Remote Jobs in 2025 |NextGen Insights
How to Network on LinkedIn - 7 Proven Tips for 2025
contentBlocks: To assist Sac State Business Administration students navigate professional development, networking and employment opportunities, this page will serve as a resource page for information, tips, and opportunities for students trying to navigate the virtual workplace environment. This page will be updated as new resources are identified.
If you are an employer or organization and would like to be featured on this page, please contact our Office of Student Engagement Director Bonnie Beevers ( bonnie.beevers@csus.edu )

Micro-Internships are short-term, paid, professional assignments that are similar to those given to new hires or interns. These projects enable Career Launchers to demonstrate skills, explore career paths, and build their networks as they seek the right full-time role.

Unlike traditional internships, Micro-Internships can take place year-round, typically range from 5 to 40 hours of work, and are due between one week and one month after kick-off. Micro-Internships are used by companies ranging from those in the Fortune 100 to emerging start-ups, and go across departments including sales, marketing, technology, HR, and finance. All Micro-Internships on the Parker Dewey platform are fixed-fee, paid engagements.

If you are launching your career, Parker Dewey provides a tremendous opportunity. Not only can you explore different career paths and work on interesting assignments, you can also demonstrate your abilities to potential employers while building your professional portfolio and network. Beyond that, your academic experience will improve as you apply what is taught in the classroom to the real world (and vice versa). Oh, did we forget to mention that you get paid for all of this?
Develop relationships with potential employers

Remote work has become the norm, offering unparalleled flexibility and the freedom to work from anywhere in the world. But finding the right platform to kickstart your remote career can be overwhelming. Click below for a curated list of the Top 15 Websites to find the best remote job opportunities in 2025
You probably already have a LinkedIn account…
You might even have some connections as part of your network from your last job or university…
But that’s far from everything you can do to take advantage of LinkedIn to build new connections and really take your career to the next level!
In fact, that’s just the tip of the iceberg.
In this guide, we’ll be going through 7+ best tips you can use to upgrade your LinkedIn profile, expand your professional connections, and network on LinkedIn like a pro.
Top 15 Websites to Find Remote Jobs in 2025 |NextGen Insights
How to Network on LinkedIn - 7 Proven Tips for 2025

pages.title: Business Pathways Networking Event | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Business Pathways Networking Event is a mini business conference for business students preparing to make the transition from campus to career. It is a networking and industry information experience for business students
headings: Expand your Networks. Engage Professionally. Be Career Ready.
Share your Expertise. Support our Students. Meet New Talent.
contentBlocks: Business Pathways is a professional development and networking event that takes place each spring semester, in the last week of February. You are highly encouraged to attend and participate in this event, and include it on your resume as a career readiness and professional development experience.

Participating in this event provides you access to industry professionals who are excited to share insights about their experience, the region and their industry. Many of these professionals are alumni who can help show you how to best prepare for a successful career in business.
While this is not a career fair, over the years this event has resulted in excellent connections that have led to internships and full-time jobs.
Students are expected to dress in professional attire and pre-register for this event.
The Business Pathways Networking Event takes place each year, early in Spring semester.
Our Spring 2026 Business Pathways Networking Event date and registration info will be published mid-fall 2025.

It is a mini business conference for business students preparing to make the transition from campus to career. It is a networking and industry information experience for business students, employers and professional associations affiliated with the business community.
Business Pathways Networking Event has two components:

Round table industry discussions: Following the keynote and lunch are two round table discussion sessions where students and professionals are given the rare opportunity to sit down for candid and helpful conversations. During the roundtable sessions, we ask professionals to represent their industry, providing relevant advice and information.

Networking Fair: The event begins and concludes with a Networking Fair, giving student participants the opportunity to get to know each company in more detail. Recruiting is always welcome but not a required component for company participation as the primary importance is placed on providing an opportunity for students to engage in professional networking.

Employers and professional organizations are encouraged to participate and support this event each year. We look to you to support this event as professionals who can provide mentoring, insight and networking opportunities for our business students. Business Pathways is designed as a career readiness and professional development event; as such it is not necessary to be in an active recruiting phase but if you are recruiting this is an excellent way to meet the talent you are looking for.
Company sponsorship are requested to present this event to our students at no cost; additionally, participating companies are invited to provide door prizes to be awarded during the event.

If you are interested in participating as a company/professional association, etc, please complete our Business Pathway Networking Interest Form and the Office of Student Engagement will reach out to you.
